skill.name: health-trend-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes health records over time to identify trends, correlations, changes, and reportable insights across symptoms, weight, medication adherence, labs, mood, and sleep.
skill.summary: Analyze personal health trends and create visual reports from local health records.
skill.author: huifer

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, key-file, reconnaissance, and obfuscation findings were reviewed as false positives caused by markdown code fences, inline code spans, Chinese text entropy, CSS selectors, and normal JavaScript examples. The confirmed concerns are privacy and supply-chain risks: the skill reads and writes sensitive local health data, and the generated HTML report loads third-party CDN scripts that execute alongside injected report data.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Scripts Run in Health Report
medium_findings.description: The report template loads Tailwind, Lucide, and ECharts from public CDNs. Because report data is injected into the same page, those scripts execute in a context that may contain sensitive health information.
medium_findings.reasoning: This is not evidence of malicious exfiltration, but it is a real supply-chain and privacy risk for generated medical reports. A compromised CDN dependency could read reportData or page content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CDN script tags and injected reportData object are directly visible in the template. The risk depends on how reports are opened and whether real health data is embedded.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Local Health Data Access and Report Writing
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to read local health records with Read, Grep, and Glob and write HTML reports under health report paths. This is expected behavior, but it handles sensitive medical data.
medium_findings.reasoning: The filesystem access is core to the skill and does not appear malicious. It still warrants a marketplace warning because generated reports may contain private health details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and examples clearly show local reads, globbing, and report output. I did not find evidence of hidden credential access or unauthorized file paths.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick alerts correspond to markdown fenced JavaScript examples, inline code spans, and JavaScript template literals, not executable shell calls.
low_findings.reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation examples, string interpolation, chart tooltip formatting, and download filename construction. No child process, shell command, eval, spawn, or system execution was found in the inspected files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple cited examples were inspected and match benign markdown or client-side JavaScript syntax. A targeted search found no exec, spawn, eval, child_process, or system calls.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Key-File Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity crypto and certificate/key detections point to ordinary text or JavaScript identifiers, such as health descriptions, mood descriptions, Object.keys, descriptiveStats, and array includes.
low_findings.reasoning: The reviewed locations do not contain MD5, SHA1, private keys, certificates, or cryptographic operations. They are health-data field examples and normal JavaScript collection operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited high-severity lines were directly checked. The scanner appears to have matched substrings inside unrelated identifiers or multilingual text.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: High entropy alerts on SKILL.md and examples.md appear to result from dense Chinese prose, symbols, tables, and formatted examples rather than encoded payloads.
low_findings.reasoning: The inspected files are readable markdown documentation. No encoded blobs, minified payloads, hidden scripts, or prompt-injection phrases were found in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entropy warning is plausible for multilingual documentation. Confidence is high because the file starts were human-readable and targeted injection scans returned no matches.

low_findings.title: CSS System-Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: System reconnaissance alerts in the stylesheet map to CSS selectors and style declarations, not operating system discovery or environment probing.
low_findings.reasoning: The cited stylesheet lines define card borders, spacing, and high-contrast print styles. They do not inspect hostnames, usernames, processes, files, or platform details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited CSS snippets are benign presentation rules. There is no semantic path from these declarations to reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CDN Dependencies in Generated Report
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated HTML reports depend on third-party JavaScript from public CDNs while displaying health analytics data.
dangerous_patterns.reasoning: External scripts are a real dependency and can execute within the report page. This should be disclosed and ideally replaced with bundled or pinned local assets for sensitive reports.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded HTTPS script sources are directly visible. The impact is contextual, so this is medium rather than high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Sensitive Health Data Processing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads personal profile, symptom, mood, diet, medication, reproductive health, allergy, radiation, and medical record files and can write an HTML report.
dangerous_patterns.reasoning: This is the intended function, but it increases privacy sensitivity and requires clear user consent and careful report handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data source list and report generation steps are explicit. I did not find evidence that data is sent to a remote endpoint by the skill itself.

content.value_statement: Health records are hard to compare across symptoms, medications, labs, mood, and sleep. This skill analyzes local health data and creates trend summaries with visual reports.
actual_capabilities: Reads local health data files for profile, symptoms, mood, diet, medication logs, reproductive health, allergies, radiation, and medical records.
Filters records by date range and defaults to a recent three-month analysis window.
Calculates trends for weight, BMI, symptoms, medication adherence, lab results, mood, and sleep.
Identifies correlations between health variables such as sleep, mood, symptoms, and medication adherence.
Generates text summaries and interactive HTML reports with ECharts visualizations.
Provides warnings, improvement notes, and follow-up suggestions based on observed patterns.
limitations: It does not provide a medical diagnosis or replace advice from a licensed clinician.
Trend and correlation quality depends on complete, consistent, and correctly dated source records.
Generated HTML reports may load external CDN scripts unless the template is modified for offline assets.
It cannot verify whether recorded symptoms, medications, or lab values are clinically accurate.

use_cases.title: Review Monthly Health Changes
use_cases.description: Summarize recent changes in weight, symptoms, mood, sleep, and lab results before a routine appointment.

use_cases.title: Track Treatment Response
use_cases.description: Compare medication adherence, symptom frequency, and lab trends after a treatment or lifestyle change.

use_cases.title: Prepare Visual Health Reports
use_cases.description: Create an interactive report that highlights important patterns, correlations, and follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Trend Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a general overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my health trends for the past three months. Summarize the main improvements, stable areas, and concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Symptom Pattern Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my symptom records from the last 90 days. Identify frequent symptoms, severity changes, possible triggers, and missing data.

prompt_templates.title: Medication and Lab Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my medication adherence with symptom frequency and lab result trends. Highlight correlations and questions to ask my clinician.

prompt_templates.title: Full Report With Correlations
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a comprehensive report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a full health trend report for the selected date range. Include weight, BMI, symptoms, medications, labs, mood, sleep, correlations, limitations, and follow-up recommendations.

output_examples.input: Analyze my health trends for the past three months.
output_examples.output: The report highlights gradual weight reduction, stable blood glucose, improved cholesterol, recurring headaches, and lower medication adherence on weekends. It recommends reviewing adherence barriers and bringing the lab trend summary to the next visit.

output_examples.input: Why do I keep getting headaches?
output_examples.output: The analysis groups headache records by time, severity, sleep, stress, meals, and medication history. It reports likely patterns, weak or strong correlations, and data gaps that limit confidence.

output_examples.input: Create a visual report for my doctor.
output_examples.output: The skill prepares an HTML report with trend charts, adherence summaries, lab result timelines, correlation heatmaps, and a disclaimer that the report is for discussion only.

best_practices: Review source files before analysis and remove records that should not appear in generated reports.
Use clear date ranges and compare similar periods when asking for trend changes.
Treat findings as discussion material and confirm medical decisions with a qualified clinician.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to self-diagnose urgent symptoms or delay emergency care.
Do not share generated HTML reports publicly if they contain personal health information.
Do not infer causation from correlations without clinical review and better evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill diagnose medical conditions?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes recorded data and trends. A licensed clinician should make diagnoses and treatment decisions.

faq.question: What data can it analyze?
faq.answer: It can use local records for profile, symptoms, mood, diet, medications, reproductive health, allergies, radiation, and medical records.

faq.question: Can it create charts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes templates for interactive HTML reports with charts for trends, adherence, labs, and correlations.

faq.question: Is my data sent to a remote service?
faq.answer: No remote upload was found in the skill instructions. However, the HTML template loads scripts from public CDNs.

faq.question: How much data is needed?
faq.answer: More consistent records produce better results. Some correlation methods need at least 30 data points.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
